title: Prompt Security, Untrusted Context, Privacy, and Authorization · Prompt Lens: Red Team Maintenance
summary: Harden a tool-using prompt that processes untrusted documents and external results. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt red team maintenance increment in a prompt threat model and red-team suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.
Validate model outputs before execution, publication, or use in security decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Red Team Maintenance
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt security injection. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt security injection. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Red Team Maintenance contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.

content.text: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside technical correctness.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt security injection. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Red Team Maintenance, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a prompt threat model and red-team suite.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A one-time red-team exercise permanently secures a prompt. This fails the Red Team Maintenance contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompting Best Practices (Current Claude model guidance) — Controls clear instructions, examples, structure, reasoning, tools, and model-specific migration cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.

steps.title: Build Red Team Maintenance evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-red-team-maintenance-lssozk" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Red Team Maintenance into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-red-team-maintenance-lssozk
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

stimulus.title: Red Team Maintenance failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A one-time red-team exercise permanently secures a prompt.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Red Team Maintenance, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.

steps.title: Defend the Red Team Maintenance model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Red Team Maintenance applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain adversarial cases and re-run them when prompts, models, tools, or data change.
